hero.headline: West Bengal Government Launches New Scheme to Support Farmers
hero.subheadline: CM Announces Subsidized Loans and Insurance for Agricultural Sector
hero.summary: The West Bengal government has launched a new scheme aimed at supporting farmers in the state. The scheme, announced by Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee, includes subsidized loans and insurance coverage for farmers. The initiative is expected to benefit over 2 million farmers in the state. The government has allocated Rs. 1,000 crore for the scheme, which will be implemented in phases. Farmers will be able to avail loans at a reduced interest rate of 4%, down from the previous 7%. The insurance component will cover crop losses due to natural calamities, pests, and diseases.
hero.impact: Farmers to receive financial support and reduced loan interest rates.

stories.headline: West Bengal Raises Minimum Wage for Unskilled Workers
stories.summary: The West Bengal government has announced an increase in the minimum wage for unskilled workers. The new wage rate will be Rs. 250 per day, up from the previous Rs. 200. The increase is part of the government's efforts to improve the living standards of workers in the state. The new wage rate will come into effect from July 1, 2026.

stories.headline: New Health Initiative to Improve Maternal and Child Health in West Bengal
stories.summary: The West Bengal government has launched a new health initiative aimed at improving maternal and child health in the state. The initiative includes the provision of free healthcare services to pregnant women and children under the age of 5. The government has allocated Rs. 500 crore for the scheme, which will be implemented in phases. The initiative is expected to benefit over 1 million women and children in the state.

stories.headline: West Bengal to Upgrade Infrastructure in Industrial Zones
stories.summary: The West Bengal government has announced plans to upgrade infrastructure in industrial zones across the state. The upgrade will include the construction of new roads, bridges, and power supply systems. The government has allocated Rs. 1,500 crore for the project, which is expected to be completed by the end of 2027. The upgrade is aimed at attracting more investments and creating job opportunities in the state.

stories.headline: West Bengal Government to Introduce New Education Policy
stories.summary: The West Bengal government is set to introduce a new education policy aimed at improving the quality of education in the state. The policy will focus on digital learning, teacher training, and infrastructure development. The government has allocated Rs. 1,000 crore for the implementation of the policy, which is expected to benefit over 10 million students in the state.

ticker: West Bengal Government Launches New Scheme to Support Farmers

state_narrative.headline: West Bengal Votes Overwhelmingly for Change
state_narrative.summary: According to state-aligned media, the recent elections in West Bengal saw a massive mandate for change, with the opposition party winning by a landslide. The reports highlighted widespread discontent with the current government and a strong desire for new leadership. Sources close to the ruling party were quoted as saying that the results were a reflection of the people's trust in the opposition's promises.
claims_made: Widespread discontent with the current government

verified_record.headline: West Bengal Elections: Mixed Results and Controversies
verified_record.summary: Independent media reported that while the opposition did make gains, the results were not as overwhelming as portrayed by state-aligned media. There were allegations of voter suppression and irregularities in certain constituencies. The Election Commission of India (ECI) was reportedly looking into these claims. The verified voter turnout was lower than the claims made by state-aligned media.
verified_record.data_check: Election Commission of India (ECI) official data

gap_analysis.key_gap: State-aligned media overstated the mandate for change and omitted allegations of voter suppression.
gap_analysis.missing_context: The context of voter suppression and lower verified voter turnout was missing in state-aligned reports.

state_narrative.headline: Farmer Protests in West Bengal: A Foreign Conspiracy
state_narrative.summary: State-aligned media reported that the recent farmer protests in West Bengal were instigated by foreign forces aiming to destabilize the region. The reports suggested that the protesters were paid and that the protests were not genuine. The state government was quoted as saying that the protests were a threat to national security.

verified_record.headline: Farmer Protests in West Bengal: Genuine Grievances Ignored
verified_record.summary: Independent media reported that the farmer protests were due to genuine grievances over new agricultural policies that adversely affected their livelihoods. There was no evidence to support the claims of foreign involvement or paid protesters. The verified data from the National Crime Records Bureau (NCRB) showed an increase in agrarian distress in the region.

gap_analysis.key_gap: State-aligned media falsely claimed foreign involvement and paid protesters, omitting genuine grievances.
gap_analysis.missing_context: The context of genuine grievances and lack of evidence for foreign involvement was missing in state-aligned reports.

stories.topic: COVID-19 Vaccination Drive in West Bengal

state_narrative.headline: West Bengal Leads in COVID-19 Vaccination Drive
state_narrative.summary: State-aligned media reported that West Bengal was leading the country in the COVID-19 vaccination drive, with the highest number of doses administered per capita. The reports highlighted the state government's efficient planning and execution, with officials claiming that the state was a model for others to follow.
claims_made: Leading the country in vaccination drive
Highest number of doses administered per capita

verified_record.headline: West Bengal's COVID-19 Vaccination Drive: Data Discrepancies
verified_record.summary: Independent media reported discrepancies in the data presented by the state government. According to the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare (MoHFW), West Bengal was not leading in the vaccination drive. The verified data showed that the state had lower vaccination rates compared to several other states. There were also reports of data manipulation by local authorities.
verified_record.data_check: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare (MoHFW) data

gap_analysis.key_gap: State-aligned media overstated West Bengal's vaccination drive, omitting data discrepancies.
gap_analysis.missing_context: The context of data discrepancies and lower verified vaccination rates was missing in state-aligned reports.

faq.question: How do I apply for Swasthya Sathi?
faq.answer:
**Swasthya Sathi** is West Bengal's universal health coverage scheme providing ₹5 lakh annual coverage per family.

**Eligibility:** All families in West Bengal with a valid ration card. The card is issued in the name of the woman head of household.

**How to apply:**
1. Visit your nearest **Duare Sarkar** camp (held periodically in every block/ward)
2. Or apply online at **swasthyasathi.gov.in**
3. Bring: Ration card, Aadhaar card, one passport photo
4. The smart card is issued on the spot at camps, or mailed within 15 days for online applications

**What's covered:** Hospitalization, surgeries, diagnostics, medicines at 1,500+ empanelled hospitals across Bengal.

**Helpline:** 1800-345-5384 (toll-free)

*(Source: Department of Health & Family Welfare, Govt. of West Bengal)*

faq.question: What is West Bengal's unemployment rate?
faq.answer:
According to the latest available data:

- **CMIE (April 2026):** West Bengal's unemployment rate is **6.8%**, marginally below the national average of 7.1%
- **PLFS 2024-25:** The state's labor force participation rate is 52.1% (male: 76.2%, female: 26.3%)
- **Youth unemployment (15-29 years):** Estimated at 18.4%, above the national average of 16.2%

**Key context:** West Bengal's female labor force participation rate (26.3%) is significantly below the national average (32.8%), indicating structural barriers to women's workforce entry.

The state's largest employers are agriculture (42%), services (36%), and manufacturing (22%). The informal sector accounts for approximately 89% of total employment.

*(Sources: CMIE Economic Outlook, Periodic Labour Force Survey 2024-25, MOSPI)*

faq.question: How do I file an RTI?
faq.answer:
**Right to Information (RTI)** allows any Indian citizen to request information from government bodies. Here's how:

**Step 1:** Write a simple application addressed to the **Public Information Officer (PIO)** of the relevant department.

**Step 2:** Include:
- Your name and address
- The specific information you're requesting (be precise)
- Write: "I request this information under the Right to Information Act, 2005"

**Step 3:** Pay ₹10 fee (₹0 for BPL card holders) via:
- Court fee stamp, postal order, or demand draft
- Online: **rtionline.gov.in** for central departments
- For WB state departments: Apply at the department office or via registered post

**Timeline:** PIO must respond within **30 days** (48 hours for life/liberty matters).

**If denied:** File a **First Appeal** to the appellate authority within 30 days. Then escalate to the **WB State Information Commission** at wbic.gov.in.

**Helpline:** WB Information Commission — 033-2214-5563

*(Source: RTI Act 2005, WB State Information Commission)*

faq.question: Explain the jute industry crisis
faq.answer:
West Bengal produces **75% of India's raw jute** and houses most of the country's jute mills, primarily along the Hooghly river belt (Howrah, Hooghly, North 24 Parganas).

**The crisis:**
- **Declining demand:** Synthetic alternatives have captured packaging markets that jute traditionally served
- **Aging infrastructure:** Most mills operate on machinery from the 1950s-70s, leading to low productivity
- **Wage disputes:** Workers earn ₹232/day — among the lowest industrial wages in India. Unions demand ₹350+
- **Mill closures:** 12 mills have closed permanently since 2019, displacing ~35,000 workers
- **Raw material price volatility:** Farmers face price crashes during peak harvest, discouraging cultivation

**Government response:**
- **Jute Packaging Material Act** mandates jute packaging for foodgrains and sugar — this keeps ~60% of mills running
- **Jute-ICARE scheme** (₹1,045 crore) for technology modernization
- **MSP for raw jute:** ₹5,350/quintal (2025-26)

**What workers can do:** Contact the Jute Workers' Helpline at the Labour Commissioner's office: 1800-345-4150.

*(Sources: Ministry of Textiles, IJMA Annual Report, The Wire, Indian Jute Mills Association)*

faq.question: What are my rights as a daily wage worker?
faq.answer:
As a daily wage or informal sector worker in West Bengal, you have these legal protections:

**Minimum Wage:**
- WB Minimum Wages Act sets floor wages by industry. Current rates range from ₹232-415/day depending on sector
- **Construction workers:** ₹356/day (skilled), ₹291/day (unskilled)
- Employers MUST pay at least minimum wage — violation is a criminal offense

**Key Rights:**
1. **Equal pay for equal work** — no gender-based wage discrimination (Article 39(d))
2. **MGNREGA:** 100 days guaranteed employment at ₹237/day. Apply at your Gram Panchayat.
3. **WB Building Workers' Welfare Board:** Register for pension (₹1,000/month), medical aid, children's education support
4. **ESI coverage** if your employer has 10+ workers and your salary is below ₹21,000/month
5. **No wage deduction** without written notice and consent

**If your wages are unpaid:**
1. File a complaint at the **District Labour Commissioner** office
2. Call the WB Labour Department helpline: **1800-345-4150**
3. You can also file a case at the **Labour Court** — no lawyer fees required

**Unions:** You have the constitutional right to form or join a trade union (Article 19(1)(c)).

*(Sources: WB Minimum Wages Act, MGNREGA Act 2005, Building & Other Construction Workers Act 1996)*

faq.question: Tell me about the MGNREGA scheme in WB
faq.answer:
**MGNREGA (Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act)** guarantees 100 days of wage employment per year to every rural household.

**In West Bengal:**
- **Wage rate:** ₹237/day (2025-26)
- **Person-days generated (2023-24):** 41.3 crore — West Bengal is among the top 5 states by utilization
- **Households benefited:** ~1.2 crore annually

**How to apply:**
1. Visit your **Gram Panchayat** office
2. Submit a written application demanding work (keep a receipt!)
3. Bring: Aadhaar card, bank passbook, passport photo
4. You'll receive a **Job Card** — this is your legal entitlement

**Your rights under MGNREGA:**
- Work must be provided **within 15 days** of application, or you're entitled to an unemployment allowance
- Wages must be paid **within 15 days** of work completion, directly to your bank account
- **Worksite facilities** are mandatory: drinking water, shade, first-aid, crèche for children
- **No contractor system** — work must be executed by the Gram Panchayat

**Common issues in WB:**
- Wage payment delays reported in several districts (average: 25-40 days vs mandated 15)
- Some panchayats show 'ghost beneficiaries' — report this via the Grievance Portal

**Grievance redressal:** nrega.nic.in/netnrega → 'Register Grievance' or call 1800-345-22-44

*(Sources: mgnregs.nic.in, Ministry of Rural Development, CAG Performance Audit 2024-25)*
